name: Club member profile with benefits and promotions
description: Response to GET /club/members?clubName=NAME5&programName=SampleProgram. Body is the documented Club Member Details schema (no JSON example in the guide; field names/casing taken verbatim from the property tables for Club Member Details, Club Member Benefits, and Club Member Promotions). Values are illustrative.

benefits.description: Free standard shipping on all club orders.

notes: Source: Loyalty Management Developer Guide v67.0 (Summer 26). Resource definition at lines 20725-20757; response schema "Club Member Details" at 28743-28770; nested "Club Member Benefits" at 28718-28739; nested "Club Member Promotions" at 28802-28826. Resource path is /club/members (no /services/data/vXX.X prefix shown in the guide; no {placeholders}, all inputs are query parameters). Documented "Available version" for the resource is 64.0; the Club Member Details/Benefits/Promotions property fields list Available Version 63.0/64.0. HTTP method is GET only; request is GET so requestExample is null. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the guide provides NO JSON example for the Club Member Details response body, only property tables. The fixture body field NAMES and CASING are taken verbatim from those tables (benefits, clubId, clubMembershipId, clubMembershipNumber, clubName, membershipStartDate, membershipEndDate, promotions; benefit* fields; promotion* fields), but all VALUES are implementer-chosen/illustrative. Field-name reconstruction from line-wrapped table cells: "club MembershipId"->clubMembershipId, "club Membership Number"->clubMembershipNumber, "membershipEnd Date"->membershipEndDate, "membership StartDate"->membershipStartDate, "benefitType Name"->benefitTypeName, "loyalty PromotionType"->loyaltyPromotionType, "member Eligibility Category"->memberEligibilityCategory, "promotion EnrollmentRqr"->promotionEnrollmentRqr. Date format (date vs datetime) is not specified by the guide and is a guess. No error/empty response example is shown in the guide, so only a success fixture is provided. Open question: exact JSON envelope (whether benefits/promotions are always present as arrays when empty) is undocumented.
